1.title: The twenty-four gold plates
1.text: Moroni's source is the twenty-four gold plates found by a search party King Limhi sent out (Mosiah 8). They had stumbled on the ruins of the destroyed Jaredite nation long before anyone could read them.

1.question: From which plates did Moroni take the account of the Jaredites?
options: The twenty-four plates found by the people of Limhi

1.question: Who wrote the original record that Moroni abridges here?
How is the long genealogy in this chapter arranged?
options: In reverse, from Ether back to the first father
By the length of each reign

1.title: Names that repeat
1.text: Across this long line, names like Com, Coriantum, and Heth appear more than once in different generations — a sign Moroni is faithfully copying a real, multigenerational record.

1.question: Who is the Moroni who abridges this record of a vanished people?
options: The son of Mormon, the last Nephite writer

1.question: Why did Moroni begin his account 'from the tower down,' not from Adam?
options: The earlier history, from creation to the tower, was already had among the Jews
It was forbidden to write of Adam

1.question: All these generations trace back to one man who came from the great tower. What was his name?

1.template: the Lord confounded the {0} of the people

1.question: What did Jared ask his brother to plead with the Lord for at the great tower?
options: That the Lord would not confound their language
For rain to end a famine
For a king to rule them

1.template: a land which is {0} above all the earth

1.question: What did the Lord promise to raise up from the seed of Jared and his brother?
options: A great nation, than which none would be greater on earth

2.question: The valley the Jaredites went down into was named after whom?

2.title: Deseret, the honey bee
2.text: Verse 3 says 'deseret, which, by interpretation, is a honey bee.' This word later named the proposed pioneer state of Deseret and remains a symbol of industry for the Latter-day Saints.

2.question: What is the everlasting decree about this choice land?
options: Whoever possesses it must serve God or be swept off when ripe in iniquity

2.question: Whom does Moroni identify as the God of this land?

2.title: Chastened for three hours
2.text: The Lord talked with the brother of Jared for three hours, chastening him because he had forgotten to call upon the name of the Lord — a striking reminder that even great prophets can neglect prayer.

2.template: the bottom thereof was tight like unto a {0}
ye shall be as a {0} in the midst of the sea

3.template: did molten out of a rock {0} small stones

3.question: What did the brother of Jared see when the Lord touched the stones?
options: The finger of the Lord, like flesh and blood

3.title: Faith that pierced the veil
3.text: The Lord says no one had ever come before Him with such great faith. Because the brother of Jared knew, 'nothing doubting,' he could not be kept from within the veil and saw the premortal Christ.

3.template: I am the {0} and the Son

3.question: What did the Lord teach about the body the brother of Jared beheld?
options: It was the body of His spirit, after which man was created
It was only a vision, not real
It was the body of an angel

3.title: The two interpreters
3.text: The two stones the Lord gives to seal up with the record are interpreters — what later scripture calls the Urim and Thummim, prepared to translate the sealed writing in due time.

3.question: After the vision of Christ, what else did the Lord show the brother of Jared?
options: All the inhabitants of the earth that had been and would be
A map of the promised land
The day of his own death

3.question: What was the brother of Jared commanded to do with the things he saw?
options: Write them and seal them up until the Lord's own time
Tell them to all the people at once

4.question: Who kept the sealed writings until Christ showed himself to his people?
When will the Lord reveal what the brother of Jared saw?
options: When people exercise faith as the brother of Jared did and become sanctified
At the creation of a new world

4.template: I am the light, and the {0}, and the truth of the world

4.title: The revelations of John
4.text: The sealed two-thirds of the plates includes a vision parallel to that of John the Revelator, to be unfolded only when Israel rends 'the veil of unbelief.'

4.question: Whose sealed revelations will be unfolded when Israel rends the veil of unbelief?

5.title: A letter to the translator
5.text: Writing around AD 421, Moroni speaks directly to whoever would one day translate the plates — words later read by Joseph Smith, more than fourteen centuries away.

5.question: To how many witnesses would the plates be shown by the power of God?

5.template: in the mouth of {0} witnesses shall these things be established

5.question: When does Moroni say his authority will be confirmed?
options: When we stand before God at the last day

6.question: What drove the Jaredite barges toward the promised land?
The watertight barges are compared to which famous vessel?
How many days were the Jaredites driven upon the water?

6.title: 344 days at sea
6.text: Buried again and again by mountain waves, lit only by glowing stones, the Jaredites were driven across the ocean for nearly a year — yet 'no monster of the sea could break them.'

6.question: What did the aging brother of Jared wish to do before he died?
options: Gather and number the people and learn what they desired

6.title: Pagag refuses the crown
6.text: Pagag, the chosen firstborn, refused to be king; and his father would not force him, commanding that no one be constrained to reign. Only then did Orihah accept.

6.question: Who finally accepted to become the first Jaredite king?

7.question: Corihor's rebellion and capture of the king fulfilled whose prophecy?
options: The brother of Jared's, that they would be brought into captivity

7.title: Moron, near Desolation
7.text: The land of Moron, seat of the Jaredite kings, lay near the land the Nephites later called Desolation — one of several hints that the two civilizations occupied overlapping ground.

7.template: made swords out of {0} for those whom he had drawn away

7.question: Who rebelled against king Shule and his own father Corihor?
How did Shule's sons save him from execution?
options: They crept into Noah's house by night, slew him, and freed their father
They fled with him by sea

7.title: Another Nimrod
7.text: A man named Nimrod appears here too — distinct from the 'mighty hunter' of the great tower whose name marked the valley in Ether 2. Jaredite parents reused the great old names.

7.question: What did the prophets sent in Shule's reign warn?
options: That the people's wickedness and idolatry would bring a curse and destruction
That a new king would rise

7.question: Why did the Lord spare the people in Shule's day?
options: Because they repented of their iniquities and idolatries

8.template: he did flatter many people, because of his {0} words

8.title: The daughter of Jared
8.text: Unnamed but 'exceedingly fair' and expert, Jared's daughter engineers the first Jaredite secret combination — reviving murderous plans she found in the ancient record.

8.question: Where did Jared's daughter get the idea for her deadly plan?
options: From the record of those of old who got kingdoms by secret plans

8.template: handed down even from {0}, who was a murderer from the beginning

8.title: Secret combinations
8.text: Moroni refuses to record the actual oaths, warning they exist 'among all people' in his future readers' day, and that any nation upholding them will be destroyed.

8.question: Why does Moroni refuse to write out the actual secret oaths?
options: They already exist among all people, including the Lamanites

8.question: Secret combinations caused the destruction of which two peoples?

8.template: it is built up by the devil, who is the father of all {0}

8.question: Why is Moroni commanded to write about these secret combinations?
options: That evil may be done away and people persuaded to do good and be saved

9.question: How was Omer warned to escape the secret combination that overthrew him?
options: In a dream, to depart out of the land
By an angel with a sword
By a prophet in the city

9.question: What did Akish do to his own son out of jealousy?
options: Shut him in prison and starved him to death

9.question: The war between Akish and his sons destroyed nearly all the people, leaving how many souls?

9.title: Cureloms and cumoms
9.text: Among the Jaredites' useful animals, verse 19 names 'cureloms and cumoms' alongside elephants — words Joseph Smith left untranslated and whose meaning remains unknown.

9.template: there were {0} and cureloms and cumoms

9.question: What did king Emer see, rejoicing in his day?

9.title: A pattern of kin-murder
9.text: Heth dethrones his father by slaying him with his own sword — repeating a pattern of murder within the family that runs through the whole Jaredite story, driven by the secret combinations.

9.question: What did the prophets warn would come in Heth's day if the people did not repent?

9.template: their flocks began to flee before the poisonous {0}

9.title: Back toward Zarahemla
9.text: The serpents drove the flocks toward the land southward that the Nephites would later call Zarahemla — another overlap between Jaredite and Nephite geography.

10.question: What kind of king was Riplakish?
options: A tyrant with heavy taxes and many wives and concubines

10.question: What happened to the tyrant Riplakish after 42 years?
options: The people rose in rebellion and killed him
He repented and freed the prisoners

10.question: King Morianton built cities and the people grew rich, but why was he cut off from the presence of the Lord?
In the days of Lib, what happened to the poisonous serpents that had plagued the land?

10.title: The narrow neck of land
10.text: Verse 20 places a great city by 'the narrow neck of land,' where the sea divides the land — the same geographic feature that would later mark the boundaries of the Nephite lands.

10.template: they did make gold, and silver, and iron, and {0}, and all manner of metals

10.question: How does Moroni describe this prosperous people under Lib and his successors?
options: Never could a people be more blessed or more prospered by the hand of the Lord
The poorest people in the land

10.question: What returned in the days of Com to threaten the kingdom?
options: Robbers who adopted the old secret oaths

11.question: What did the prophets in the days of Com prophesy?
options: The destruction of the people unless they repented
The coming of a new king

11.template: their bones should become as heaps of {0}

11.question: How did the people respond to the prophets in the days of Ethem?
options: They hardened their hearts; the prophets mourned and withdrew
They crowned the prophets as kings

11.question: The mighty man who overthrew Moron was a descendant of whom?
What did the prophets foretell would happen if the people did not repent?
options: The Lord would bring forth another people to possess the land
The sea would cover the land

11.title: Ether is born in captivity
11.text: Coriantor begets Ether while dwelling all his days in captivity. Out of that captivity comes the prophet who would write the record of his entire vanished people.

12.title: The tireless prophet
12.text: Ether preached 'from the morning, even until the going down of the sun' and could not be restrained because of the Spirit of the Lord in him — even as the people rejected him.

12.question: What does the hope that comes of faith become to the souls of men?

12.title: The trial of faith
12.text: Verse 6 gives one of scripture's most-quoted definitions: 'faith is things which are hoped for and not seen' — and 'ye receive no witness until after the trial of your faith.'

12.template: faith is things which are hoped for and not {0}

pairs.right: The prison tumbled to the earth
A great miracle among the Lamanites

12.question: What happened to those whose faith was so exceedingly strong, even before Christ came?
options: They could not be kept from within the veil
They were taken to heaven without dying

12.question: What weakness did Moroni fear the Gentiles would mock?

12.template: I give unto men {0} that they may be humble
this love which thou hast had for the children of men is {0}

12.question: As he bids farewell, Moroni says that at the judgment his garments will not be spotted with what?
options: With the blood of the Gentiles
With the gold of the world
With the tears of the righteous
With the dust of the earth

12.question: What does Moroni commend in his final words of this chapter?
options: To seek this Jesus of whom the prophets and apostles have written
To keep the law of Moses

13.question: What did Ether prophesy this land was the place of?
options: The New Jerusalem, which should come down out of heaven

13.question: The New Jerusalem on this land would be built for the remnant of whose seed?

13.template: whose garments are white through the blood of the {0}

13.question: Where did Ether hide after being cast out, to write his record?
options: In the cavity of a rock

13.title: Writing by night
13.text: Hidden in the cavity of a rock, Ether came out by night to watch the destruction falling upon the people, and that is how he composed the remainder of his record.

13.question: Who among the people repented as the wars began?
options: No one — none of the fair sons and daughters repented

13.title: The strange prophecy about Coriantumr
13.text: Ether prophesied that if Coriantumr would not repent, everyone would die except himself — who would live to see another people inherit the land and be buried by them. It came to pass exactly.

13.question: What did Ether prophesy would happen to Coriantumr alone if the people did not repent?
options: He alone would survive, see another people inherit the land, and be buried by them
He would reign a thousand years
He would be taken to heaven
He would regain the whole kingdom

13.question: In the valley of Gilgal, what did Coriantumr finally do to Shared?
options: He beat him and slew him

13.question: Though Coriantumr slew Shared, what did Shared do to him?
options: Wounded him in the thigh, so he did not battle for two years

14.title: The curse returns
14.text: The curse on the land returns as in the beginning: tools and swords laid aside 'became slippery' and could not be found the next day, so every man kept his sword in his hand.

14.question: What was the great curse on the land in this time of war?
options: Tools and swords laid down vanished and could not be found

14.template: Lib was a man of great {0}, more than any other man

14.question: What did Lib do to Coriantumr in battle?
options: Smote his arm so he was wounded, but Coriantumr's army pressed forward

14.question: Who was Shiz, who pursued Coriantumr and burned cities?

14.template: Behold, he sweepeth the {0} before him

14.title: Too many dead to bury
14.text: The war was so swift and total that there was no one left to bury the dead: the bodies of men, women, and children lay strewn across the land, and the very scent of it troubled the living.

14.question: Why did Shiz relentlessly pursue Coriantumr?
options: He had sworn to avenge the blood of his brother
He was seeking the sacred records

14.question: In the battle by the hill Comnor, what happened to Coriantumr?
options: Shiz gave him many deep wounds; he fainted and was carried off as if dead

14.question: Why did Shiz command his people not to pursue Coriantumr's armies?
options: The loss of life on both sides had been too great

15.template: there had been slain by the sword already nearly two {0} of his people

15.title: Coriantumr's repentance comes too late
15.text: Only after nearly two million dead did Coriantumr begin to remember the words of the prophets and to repent — too late to stop the destruction he himself had fed.

15.question: What does the name of the waters of Ripliancum mean?

15.title: Ramah is Cumorah
15.text: The hill Ramah, where the Jaredites gathered for their final battle, is the same hill Moroni calls Cumorah — where his father Mormon hid the records. Two civilizations end at the very same place.

15.template: it was that same hill where my father {0} did hide up the records

15.question: Why did the people go again to battle despite their grief?
options: The Spirit of the Lord had ceased striving and Satan had full power over their hearts

15.template: even as a man who is drunken with {0}

15.title: The countdown
15.text: Moroni records the annihilation as an exact countdown: 52 against 69, then 32 against 27, until only two men remain standing over the dead of an entire nation.

15.question: How did the final duel between Coriantumr and Shiz end?
options: Coriantumr smote off the head of Shiz

15.question: What did Ether do at the Lord's command after the destruction?
options: Went forth, saw all fulfilled, finished his record, and hid it
